meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code Fast: Git with AI Tutorial for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, build your portfolio, and master your first project.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary hurdle for every developer. By integrating Git with AI, you transform your codebase into an interactive, self-documenting system. Use Claude Code for deep analysis and Antigravity to manage version context, allowing you to isolate errors, revert broken states, and solve complex logic bugs with surgical precision.
content.intro:
You’ve been staring at the monitor for three hours. The cursor blinks with rhythmic cruelty, mocking a function that refuses to return the correct value. You’re not just stuck; you’re hitting the wall that separates casual coders from professional engineers. The difference isn't raw intelligence—it's having the right workflow to translate confusion into clarity.

Welcome to the new era of software development. By leveraging AI-powered Git workflows, you no longer debug in isolation. You now have a partner that understands your commit history, branch logic, and intent. This guide will show you how to stop fighting your code and start commanding it, turning every error into a milestone for your portfolio.

steps.title: Establish a Clean Git Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, you must ensure your environment is clean. We use Git to capture the 'known good' state so we can experiment fearlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory and suggest a .gitignore file that prevents bloat while keeping essential configuration files.

steps.title: Contextualize Bugs with Claude Code
steps.description: Feed your error logs directly into the AI to gain structural insights into why the application is failing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic error in this file based on the provided traceback logs. Identify the specific line causing the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Analyze the latest error logs in /logs and compare them against the functions in src/core."

steps.title: Isolate Changes with Atomic Commits
steps.description: Never debug in the dark. Make small, atomic commits so that if an AI suggestion breaks something, you can pinpoint the exact change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise, conventional commit message for the fix I am about to apply to this specific module.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'fix: resolve null pointer in user auth logic'

steps.title: Iterative Patching and Verification
steps.description: Use the AI to write test cases that verify the fix. This builds professional habits and ensures your code is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test for the fix we just implemented to ensure the bug does not regress in future updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Create a jest test file that validates the fix we just applied to the auth service."

steps.title: Merge and Document Your Learning
steps.description: Finalize your fix and document the resolution in your Git history, adding immense value to your portfolio documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process we just went through into a professional README.md update for my project.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout main && git merge fix/debug-session-001

pro_tips: Always run 'git diff' before asking AI for help; the AI needs to see exactly what changed to provide an accurate diagnosis.
Use 'git bisect' in tandem with AI to find the exact commit that introduced a bug if your project is large.
Treat your Git commit messages as documentation for your future self; include the 'Why' of a fix, not just the 'What'.
If the AI suggests a major refactor, commit your current state first. It allows you to revert back if the refactor introduces new side effects.
Use AI to write your 'git log' summaries periodically; this creates a beautiful, professional narrative for your GitHub profile.
content.next_steps: Now that you can debug with confidence, start building a larger feature set. Use this Git-AI workflow to manage feature branches and document your progress in your public GitHub repository to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to dominate your first project. Stop letting bugs stall your progress. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
